project_overview.project_name: Honduras Early Childhood Education Improvement Project
project_overview.project_development_objective: To improve (a) SE's institutional capacity for preschool management; (b) teaching practices of preschool teachers and Educators; and (c) physical learning environments in preschools serving children in Targeted Areas
project_overview.total_financing: US$39.5 million (IDA Credit: US$30 million; GPE Grant: US$9.5 million)
project_overview.beneficiaries: Approximately 178,400 preschool students (80% of total preschool students); 7,900 teachers in CEPB and Educators in CCEPREB (65% of total); SE staff including SDGEPB, UPEG, DGDP, and DIGECEBI

geographic_scope.coverage_type: National with targeted focus on 6 most disadvantaged departments and 40 most disadvantaged municipalities
geographic_scope.fragile_or_conflict_affected_areas: Partial - Honduras experiences high rates of violence and crime affecting children's development; project includes violence prevention measures and addresses security considerations in school settings

key_components.component_name: Component 1: Strengthening SE's Institutional Capacity for Preschool Management
key_components.description: Strengthen SE's enabling conditions for sustainable preschool management including design/implementation of Preschool Education Management Model (PEM), community participation through Municipal Councils (COMDEs), human resources capacity building, and planning/monitoring systems improvement
key_components.financing: US$5.75 million (IDA: US$3.785 million; GPE: US$1.965 million)

key_components.component_name: Component 2: Updating Curricular Design and Improving Teaching Practices
key_components.description: Update preschool curriculum for all service modalities, provide learning materials, implement in-service teacher training program (Diplomado), provide pedagogical coaching, and establish measurement tools for child development and learning environment quality
key_components.financing: US$17.15 million (IDA: US$9.615 million; GPE: US$7.535 million)

key_components.component_name: Component 3: Improving Physical Learning Environments in Targeted Areas
key_components.description: Upgrade building codes and infrastructure standards; construct, expand, and rehabilitate 224 classrooms in preschools serving children in targeted areas with climate-resilient design and low-carbon technologies
Support PIU staffing and operations, establish fund tracking systems, conduct audits, and hire Independent Verification Agents for DLI verification
Provide immediate response to eligible emergencies through reallocation of funds from other components

implementation_arrangements.implementing_agency: Ministry of Education (Secretaría de Educación - SE) through a Project Implementation Unit (PIU) located within the Preschool Education Subdirectorate (SDGEPB)
implementation_arrangements.coordination_mechanisms: Executive Committee chaired by Under-Secretary of Technical-Pedagogical Affairs; coordination with Local Education Group (LEG), Donor Group for Education (MERECE), NGOs, and civil society; liaison with National Education Council (CNE) for PESE monitoring; coordination with Ministry of Finance (SEFIN)
implementation_arrangements.procurement_approach: World Bank Procurement Regulations for IPF Borrowers; use of STEP system; prior review for high-value contracts; open-national and open-international competitive bidding; standard bidding documents; procurement plan covering first 18 months prepared
implementation_arrangements.monitoring_and_evaluation: Results Framework with PDO and intermediate indicators; M&E Unit within PIU responsible for data collection; Independent Verification Agent for DLI verification; semi-annual interim unaudited financial reports; annual audits; coordination with SE's UPEG and CNE; training for SE staff on M&E; sustainability strategy for M&E capacity by mid-term review

identified_risks.risk_description: Honduras suffers persistent weakness in education system regarding transparency, governance, and accountability; ongoing sector uncertainty due to GoH reform efforts; frequent teacher strikes and protests causing disruptions
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Broad consultations and interactions generating political commitment at highest level; broad support from local stakeholders; involvement of MERECE, LEG, and government stakeholders in monitoring

identified_risks.risk_description: Increased fiscal deficit due to major public investments and reduced revenues; considerable debt obligations limiting public expenditures in education; limited fiscal space
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: SE budget has steadily increased from 2015-2019; GoH commitment to PESE 2018-2030; GoH commitment to absorb costs of newly appointed staff; introduction of DLIs requiring achievement of results before reimbursement

identified_risks.risk_description: ECE is priority in PESE but need to establish monitoring mechanisms for broader educational goals; funding and implementation concerns
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Project supports interventions to better manage and monitor ECE subsector; World Bank will keep Coordinating and Finance Ministries informed of concerns; coordination with MERECE and LEG

identified_risks.risk_description: GPE requires results-based financing (30% of funding) - new disbursement modality for GoH; complexity of DLI-based disbursement arrangements
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Close coordination with GPE during project design; GPE involved in preparation process; project design aligned with PESE and SE's Institutional Strategic Plan; cost-effective, evidence-based activities; balance between realism and ambition; clear expectations and adequate resource allocation

identified_risks.risk_category: Institutional Capacity for Implementation and Sustainability
identified_risks.risk_description: SE has limited technical, management, coordination and operational capacity; limited experience with World Bank procedures and DLI modality; limited local personnel with fiduciary management experience
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Establishment of PIU with necessary personnel; specific interventions to strengthen SE's institutional capacity; small committee of ministerial authorities for coordination; World Bank procurement and FM specialists providing close support; fiduciary training; sustainability strategy to integrate PIU roles into SE by mid-term review

identified_risks.risk_description: SE has limited knowledge of World Bank fiduciary processes and instruments; no previous experience with results-based financing modality; limited local personnel with relevant fiduciary experience
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Close support from World Bank procurement and FM specialists during implementation; fiduciary training and team building; concrete processes for monitoring and accounting of evidence-based activities; quarterly supervision during first year; at least two FM supervision missions per year; desk reviews of interim reports and audit reports

identified_risks.risk_description: High incidence of violence in Honduras including community and gang violence could disrupt school services and children's attendance; four in 10 Hondurans aged 18-24 experienced violence during childhood
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Government monitoring and adjustment of intervention locations as needed; communication campaigns to engage beneficiaries and promote school attendance; project financing school crime and violence prevention training for teachers; inclusion of violence prevention content in teacher training; positive discipline and communication training for parents, teachers, and community leaders

identified_risks.risk_description: Risk of teacher turnover due to inadequate working conditions; high rates of violence disrupting service provision; fiscal sustainability concerns
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Development of sustainable strategy to recruit, select, motivate, and retain teaching talent; analysis of Educators' working conditions with recommendations; improvement of non-salary working conditions through coaching, materials, and better environments; violence prevention measures to strengthen children's resilience; evidence-informed interventions for violence prevention

triggered_safeguard_policies: Assessment and Management of Environmental and Social Risks and Impacts (ESS1)
Resource Efficiency and Pollution Prevention and Management (ESS4)
Land Acquisition, Restrictions on Land Use and Involuntary Resettlement (ESS5)
Biodiversity Conservation and Sustainable Management of Living Natural Resources (ESS6)
Indigenous Peoples/Sub-Saharan African Historically Underserved Traditional Local Communities (ESS7)
safeguards_and_social.grievance_redress_mechanism: Yes - Project includes Grievance Redress Mechanism (GRM) at project level; World Bank's Grievance Redress Service (GRS) available for project-affected communities; World Bank Inspection Panel available for complaints regarding WB non-compliance; GRM includes code of conduct on construction sites and redress mechanism for grievances from adversely affected individuals
safeguards_and_social.gender_considerations: No identifiable gender gaps in preschool enrollment; project includes activities on gender and inclusive education; curriculum will include content on gender and resilience to violence; in-service training includes content on gender biases, inclusive education, and violence prevention (including gender-based); project supports professional development and improved working conditions for teachers (majority women); construction sites will have measures to address sexual misconduct; works ensure safe access for all populations including those with disabilities
vulnerable_groups: Indigenous and Afro-descendant children (22% attend CEPB/CCEPREB; 85% live in poverty in isolated rural areas)
Children in lowest income quintiles (61% enrollment vs 91% in highest quintile)
Children in poorest regions experiencing reductions in enrollment
Children exposed to violence (4 in 10 aged 18-24 experienced violence; 1/3 suffered physical violence; 13% experienced sexual violence)
Children in rural and remote areas
Children affected by climate-related hazards (floods, droughts, landslides)

fcv_relevant_details.conflict_sensitivity: Project addresses violence prevention through: inclusion of violence prevention content in teacher training; positive discipline and communication training for parents, teachers, and community leaders; identification of distress in children and empowerment to respond; identification of victimization hotspots on way to school; safety measures around school buildings; evidence-informed violence prevention interventions; communication campaigns to promote school attendance despite violence; project design includes hands-on approach to violence prevention
fcv_relevant_details.fragility_context: Honduras characterized by: high poverty rates (17.2% extreme poor; 61.9% living on less than US$5.50/day - second poorest in Western Hemisphere after Haiti); high income inequality (Gini 0.50); high rates of violence and crime affecting children's development; vulnerability to natural disasters (earthquakes, hurricanes, droughts, floods, landslides); climate change increasing frequency/severity of hazards; low human capital (HCI rank 103/157); learning crisis with only 29.7% achieving minimum proficiency in reading, 15.4% in math; large disparities in access and quality of education by socioeconomic status and geography; weak governance and accountability in education sector; low adaptive capacity at national, regional, local levels
fcv_relevant_details.security_considerations: Project includes: safety of schools as consideration in infrastructure design; accessibility measures; natural disaster risk considerations; overall community safety during construction; climate resilience design and low-carbon technologies for infrastructure; occupational health and safety plan; code of conduct on construction sites; grievance redress mechanism for construction-related issues; training on school crime and violence prevention for teachers; positive discipline approaches; community engagement on school safety
fcv_relevant_details.displacement_or_refugees: No - Project does not involve land acquisition or involuntary resettlement; however, ESS5 (Land Acquisition, Restrictions on Land Use and Involuntary Resettlement) is triggered as precautionary measure; project works within existing school premises; project notes seasonal migration of families within and outside country for employment opportunities as demand-side challenge to preschool attendance
